cross_scene_arcs.title: Mastery of Survival Through Adaptation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chuck progresses from struggling with basic survival tasks to reliably procuring food and sustaining fire, demonstrating increasing skill and self-sufficiency.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: inexperienced and injured while attempting to open a coconut
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: efficiently spearing fish with practiced precision
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tools and methods remain physically taxing and isolating

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emotional Isolation and Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chuck moves from emotional numbness to forming attachments with objects, then reestablishes meaningful human connections after rescue, culminating in shared understanding and recognition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: alone and scavenging, briefly reengaging with joy through play
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: laughing and connecting naturally with others in post-rescue life
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to fully integrate the depth of his isolation into daily relationships

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Stranded Survivor to Purposeful Return
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chuck evolves from disoriented castaway to active planner of escape, eventually returning to society and being recognized for his perseverance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: dazed and thirsty, collecting packages without purpose
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: acknowledged for persistence and granted access to a private home
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: uncertain future role in a world that has moved on

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Loss and Meaning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chuck grapples with the irreversible loss of Wilson and the emotional void left by years of isolation, leading to a renewed commitment to human connection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: collapsing in grief after losing Wilson at sea
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: affirming that humans are not meant to be alone
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to rebuild a life that sustains emotional intimacy

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unfulfilled Commitment and Emotional Avoidance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kelly seeks emotional and symbolic commitment from Chuck, but he consistently avoids deep engagement, prioritizing work and emotional withdrawal, culminating in the breakup when she demands a ring and he offers luggage instead.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Romantic relationship strained by Chuck's absence and emotional hesitation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Relationship ends due to Chuck's failure to meet Kelly's need for commitment
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Chuck's inability to express love or make lasting commitments undermines the relationship

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reconnection and Emotional Aftermath of Absence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After years of separation, Kelly confronts the reality of Chuck's return, having already moved on by marrying Gary and raising a daughter; their final interactions provide emotional closure but confirm they now live separate lives.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Kelly heartbroken over Chuck's repeated departures and emotional unavailability
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kelly reaffirms love but returns to her family in Memphis, accepting that their relationship belongs to the past
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Chuck's return reopens old wounds or allows both to finally move forward

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Ketch as Symbol of Lost Possibility
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The chartered ketch represents a shared dream between Chuck and Kelly that never materializes; it resurfaces in fantasy, memory, and farewell, symbolizing what was lost due to absence and indecision.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shared vacation plans reflect unfulfilled intimacy and future aspirations
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ketch remembered as part of what Kelly buried with Chuck, marking the end of their shared future
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The dream remains unrealized, underscoring the cost of Chuck's emotional and physical absence

cross_scene_arcs.title: Kelly's Transition to a New Life
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kelly builds a stable life with her husband Gary and daughter Hannah, moving from emotional entanglement with Chuck to a grounded domestic reality that excludes him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Kelly learns Chuck is alive and confronts the return of a past life
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kelly resumes her role as wife and mother in Memphis, fully integrated into a new family
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How she reconciles her enduring feelings for Chuck with her current responsibilities

cross_scene_arcs.title: Operational Authority and Conflict Over Systemic Rules
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stan enforces FedEx's systemic priorities by canceling Chuck's flight, sparking initial conflict, then gradually shifts toward mentorship by justifying his decision and encouraging Chuck to embrace risk, culminating in delegating full responsibility to him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stan asserts authority by enforcing connection policy despite Chuck's dedication
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stan steps back from direct oversight, trusting Chuck to operate independently
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension between individual effort and systemic rules in logistics operations

cross_scene_arcs.title: Defense of Field Autonomy vs. Corporate Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stan consistently supports field-driven decisions, defending Chuck's local management choice in St. Petersburg and later enabling his personal investigation, positioning himself as a champion of operational autonomy against centralized control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stan defends Chuck's field decision in a corporate meeting
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stan uses his authority to enable Chuck's deeper investigation into the package
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: ongoing conflict between local operational judgment and corporate preference for centralized control

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emotional Reconnection After Presumed Death
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stan reacts with anger to a prank call about Chuck's death, then experiences shock and relief when Chuck reappears, leading to an emotional reunion and Stan's active support during Chuck's reintegration into public life.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stan is angered by a mocking call implying Chuck's death
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stan guides Chuck through his return, acting as a stabilizing presence
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Stan hesitates to fully disclose what he knows about Kelly

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ideological Divergence on Purpose and Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stan represents the pre-island win-or-lose mindset, clashing with Chuck's transformed understanding that survival involves simultaneous gain and loss, particularly as Chuck insists on a personal mission that defies corporate logic.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stan toasts to a competitive worldview Chuck now rejects
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stan questions Chuck's personal delivery quest, highlighting their ideological divide
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Stan can truly understand Chuck's transformed worldview
